0:
Vuyargur has restocking on the agenda.

※Please note that the difficulty of this quest has been synced to your current level. Furthermore, you may not proceed with a class or job that is different from when you accepted this quest.

1: Speak with the Yok Huy supplier at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

2: Deliver the requested items to the Yok Huy supplier.

24: Speak with the Yok Huy supplier at Zirgorteh the Open-armed.

48: I'm told the restoration workers residing on the mountain are running low on critical materials. We've a mind to replenish a few daily necessities, if you're willing to contribute.

49: Our supplier at Warmth has but recently taken inventory. He should be able to give you a more detailed report of what we currently lack.

50: The workers tell me our stock of daily necessities is dwindling yet again. I'd like to resupply our stores as soon as possible─with your invaluable aid, of course.

51: In light of this, I imagine the supplier at Warmth has already taken inventory. He should be able to inform you of the goods we currently lack.

52: With so many restoration workers choosing to dwell here, the demand for living necessities increases by the day... That said, we can barely keep up.

53: We're sorely in need of silverware, so I'd like to make that our first priority. I've already prepared the necessary materials─you are free to take more from the crate there, should you require it.

54: We're sorely in need of cleaning cloths, so I'd like to make that our first priority. I've already prepared the necessary materials─you are free to take more from the crate there, should you require it.

55: We're sorely in need of medicine, so I'd like to make that our first priority. I've already prepared the necessary materials─you are free to take more from the crate there, should you require it.

56: The crate appears to be filled to the brim with sundry materials─none of which you require at this time.

59: You obtain additional crafting materials from the crate.

62: Ah, you're back. The crafting went smoothly, I take it?

63: Let's see here... Yes, this is just enough! On behalf of our workers, I offer my thanks.

64: Your presence is a gift to us, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, and I am sure Vuyargur is of the same mind. I wager he's just as eager to hear of your efforts, so I'll leave you to report the good news to him.

65: Thank you again, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. Now, you'd best be off to Vuyargur. Wouldn't want to keep him waiting, would you?

66: The restocking went smoothly? Excellent! Many thanks to you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. The journey from the peaks to Worlar's Echo is a lengthy one, which makes replenishing our supplies a rather thorny task...

67: Until we are fully self-sufficient, your aid will be vital to us, just as it was today. It is my hope that we can count on you again when next our stores grow scarce.
